records.ety: [OE. beforen, biforen, before, AS. beforan; pref. be- + foran, fore, before. See Be-, and Fore.]

defs: In front of; preceding in space; ahead of; as, to stand before the fire; before the house.
quotes: His angel, who shall go Before them in a cloud and pillar of fire.

defs: Preceding in time; earlier than; previously to; anterior to the time when; -- sometimes with the additional idea of purpose; in order that.
quotes: Before this treatise can become of use, two points are necessary.
notes: ☞ Formerly before, in this sense, was followed by that. “Before that Philip called thee . . . I saw thee.”

defs: An advance of; farther onward, in place or time.
quotes: The golden age . . . is before us.

defs: Prior or preceding in dignity, order, rank, right, or worth; rather than.
quotes: He that cometh after me is preferred before me.
The eldest son is before the younger in succession.

defs: In presence or sight of; face to face with; facing.
quotes: Abraham bowed down himself before the people.
Wherewith shall I come before the Lord?

defs: Under the cognizance or jurisdiction of.
quotes: If a suit be begun before an archdeacon.

defs: Open for; free of access to; in the power of.
quotes: The world was all before them where to choose.

defs: On the fore part; in front, or in the direction of the front; -- opposed to in the rear.
quotes: The battle was before and behind.
You tell me, mother, what I knew before.
When the butt is out, we will drink water; not a drop before.
notes: ☞ Before is often used in self-explaining compounds; as, before-cited, before-mentioned; beforesaid.
